question_text: A 2000 N car must be lifted by a hydraulic jack. The jack has a small piston of area 0.01 m² and a large piston of area 0.1 m². What is the minimum pressure needed in the system to lift the car?
explanation: To lift the car, the pressure must create an upward force of at least 2000 N on the large piston. Using Pressure = Force ÷ Area: P = 2000 N ÷ 0.1 m² = 20,000 Pa. This same pressure acts throughout the hydraulic fluid, making hydraulic systems very effective.
